<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I need to create a `Directory.Packages.props` file with Central Package Management. Let me analyze the versions:

- `System.Text.Json`: 10.0.1 (Api, Shared, Tests) vs 8.0.4 (Worker) → highest: 10.0.1
- `Azure.Identity`: 1.13.2 (Api) vs 1.11.4 (Shared) → highest: 1.13.2
- `Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting`: 8.0.1 (Worker only)
- `OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting`: 1.15.0 (Worker only)


- `xunit`: 2.9.3 (Tests only)

I'll set up the central package management file with all these versions defined, then remove the version attributes from each project's package references.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
